document.title: Digital Factory — Business Requirements Specification
document.language_note: Requirements are stated as business rules in operational language, to be converted by engineering into technical specifications. Preferences expressed are business rules, not design instructions.

system.description: Manufacturing Execution System with full per-unit traceability built in from day one. System of record for what was built, how, by whom, and whether it is fit to ship.

scale.concurrency: multiple units, stations, and operators simultaneously

principles.statement: The order is the authoritative definition of what a unit should be; allocation, assembly verification, and gates 9-11 validate the built unit against its order.
principles.rationale: Single source of truth prevents the gates from disagreeing.

principles.statement: Strict by default; controlled override only at the right authority level; always logged with who/when/why.
Hard-stops cannot be clicked past by operators. A blocked step shows a clear reason and cannot proceed at the bench.
Every override, authorization, waiver, re-allocation, and gate result carries the role-holder identity and is retained in the unit's permanent record.
principles.rationale: Makes role tiers meaningful and the record auditable.

principles.statement: The system must be extensible without re-engineering for new serial-worthy sub-assembly types and new device model recipes; adding either is a configuration/data action, not a code change.
The unit's permanent record (genealogy, calibration certificate, gate results, full history) is the irreplaceable artifact of a regulated instrument and must be protected accordingly.

tracked_unit.unit_of_work: individual device plus its serial-worthy sub-assemblies, married into a single final device serial (the genealogy serial)
tracked_unit.serial_lot_ownership: Inventory Management module (external); factory consumes/binds serial+lot across an interface and does not own the inventory record

stages.name: Order created
stages.description: Customer customizes and orders the device on the eStore.

stages.name: Order received
stages.description: Approved order handed to the factory and validated at the boundary before admission.

controls.trigger: missing required field OR unknown model (no recipe)
controls.action: reject order; report reason to upstream system

stages.name: Parts allocated
stages.description: Specific serial numbers reserved for the order; inventory operator delivers those exact units.

controls.action: supervisor re-allocates replacement serial of same type; log who/when/why/old_serial/new_serial; retain both in genealogy
controls.on_release: report release reason code to inventory module; factory must NOT return part to available stock on its own authority

stages.name: Assembly
stages.description: Assembler scans each serialized part at the moment of assembly; scan is a verification gate (not part selection); successful scan marries the serial to the device genealogy.

controls.note: Part correctness is enforced upstream by the inventory operator; bench scan catches anything wrong that reached the station.

stages.name: Software & firmware install
stages.description: Install the same pinned, validated factory baseline on every device; device reaches a known identical state before networking; no cloud identity yet.

stages.name: Software & firmware update from cloud
stages.description: Unit must confirm it holds the latest production SW/FW from the cloud before passing.

controls.action: hard-block affected units until cloud returns
controls.decision_note: Production throughput is intentionally coupled to cloud availability at stage 7. A cloud or connectivity outage halts all affected units with no override. Accepted operational tradeoff to guarantee every shipped unit carries the latest production SW/FW. Chosen deliberately.

derived_requirements.statement: Monitor cloud/connectivity availability and alert the floor immediately when stage 7 is blocked and why.

stages.name: Cloud provisioning
stages.description: Device enrolled with the cloud and issued its permanent cloud identity/credentials.

stages.name: Hardware checks / setup
stages.description: Configuration-driven hardware checks against the ordered model recipe; verify the built unit matches its order; flag missing/unexpected hardware.

controls.fail_action: route to hardware rework (build/component fix), then re-enter stage 9

stages.name: Calibration
stages.description: Calibrate against certified reference standards; capture full reproducible record.

controls.trigger: any reference standard expired or lacking a valid certificate

controls.on_pass: record passing run on certificate; retain all attempts internally

stages.certificate_policy: Shipped certificate shows only the valid passing run; full internal record retains all attempts.

stages.name: Quality Control
stages.description: Final human sign-off on both the physical unit and its complete record.

controls.fail_action: route to QC rework/disposition, then re-enter stage 11
controls.pass_action: finalize record and authorize unit for stage 12 and shipping
controls.separation_of_duty: QC signer cannot be the person who did the work on that unit (see RBAC-3, RBAC-4)

stages.name: Cloud backup
stages.description: Back up the unit's complete factory record to the cloud before packaging/shipping.

controls.action: hard-block unit; cannot proceed to packaging/shipping until backed up
controls.decision_note: As at stage 7, the line is intentionally coupled to cloud availability. A device must not leave the floor until its irreplaceable record is preserved off-site. Accepted deliberately.

stages.name: Package
stages.description: Packaging; separable into a future fulfillment system.

stages.name: Ship
stages.description: Record packaged/shipped status with timestamp and operator; attach final shipping documents and calibration certificate; factory lifecycle complete at 'shipped'.

UNIT-1.statement: The system tracks an individual device and its serial-worthy sub-assemblies, married into a single final device serial (genealogy serial).

UNIT-2.statement: Serial-worthy sub-assemblies married into the genealogy: x-ray tube, detector, DPP, SOM, MCB (carrier board), battery pack, BMS board, chassis. List must be extensible.

UNIT-3.statement: Serial numbers and lot information are owned by the Inventory Management module; the factory consumes/binds serial+lot across an interface and does not own the inventory record.
UNIT-3.rationale: Keeps the inventory module independently designable.

ORD-1.statement: An approved order must contain at minimum: order identifier, model/variant, chosen options/flags, quantity, approval marker (who approved, when).

ORD-2.statement: Validate an incoming order at the boundary; reject any order missing a required field or naming an unknown model; report the rejection reason upstream.
ORD-2.rationale: Don't let a malformed or un-buildable order onto the floor.

ORD-3.statement: An order for a model with no known recipe must be rejected; a model recipe must exist before an order for that model can be admitted.

ORD-4.statement: Logistics information (destination, carrier, billing, tracking) is NOT required on the order and is out of scope (reserved for fulfillment).

ALLOC-1.statement: Allocation reserves specific serial numbers for the order; the inventory operator delivers those exact units to the bench.
ALLOC-1.rationale: Parts are QC'ed at incoming before entering inventory, so every serial in stock is known-good; makes strict serial-level allocation workable with strongest traceability.

ALLOC-2.statement: When an allocated serial cannot be used, a supervisor may re-allocate a replacement serial of the same type; the swap must be logged (who, when, why, old serial, new serial).

ALLOC-3.statement: Both the original (released) serial and its replacement must remain in the unit's genealogy/history; re-allocation does not erase the original.

ALLOC-4.statement: Re-allocation is not automatic and does not halt the order; it requires supervisor authorization.
ALLOC-4.rationale: Silent auto-swaps lose the trail; halting entirely is too brittle at 10+/day.

ALLOC-5.statement: On release of a serial, report the release reason code (damaged/lost/failed-at-bench/reprioritized) to the Inventory module and let it decide the part's fate; the factory must NOT return a part to available stock on its own authority.
ALLOC-5.rationale: Factory knows why the swap happened, but judging the part is inventory's job.

ASM-1.statement: At each assembly scan, hard-stop (block step, clear reason, no operator override) on: wrong part type, wrong/unallocated serial, already-used serial, out-of-sequence, unknown serial.

ASM-2.statement: No override of an assembly hard-stop at the bench; correctness of delivered parts is enforced upstream by the inventory operator; the bench scan is the verification.

ASM-3.statement: A successful assembly scan binds (marries) that specific serial to the device's genealogy.

SW-1.statement: Every device receives the same pinned, validated factory SW/FW baseline at install; the device reaches a known identical state before networking.
SW-1.rationale: Rules out mystery software if calibration/test later fails; clean audit answer to what was originally installed.

SW-2.statement: At install, the device does not yet have a unique cloud identity; it carries only the generic validated baseline.

SW-3.statement: A unit cannot pass stage 7 until it has confirmed it holds the latest production SW/FW from the cloud.

SW-4.statement: If the cloud is unreachable at stage 7, affected units are hard-blocked and wait until the cloud returns; no override path.

SW-5.statement: Monitor cloud/connectivity availability and alert the floor immediately when stage 7 is blocked and why.

PROV-1.statement: The device's permanent cloud identity is created at provisioning (stage 8) when the device is enrolled and issued operational credentials.

PROV-2.statement: The cloud identity is a separate credential (keys/certificate), distinct from the genealogy serial, bound one-to-one to it.

PROV-3.statement: The cloud credential must be independently rotatable; if compromised or expired, the cloud re-issues a new credential bound to the same unchanging genealogy serial. The genealogy serial must never change.
PROV-3.rationale: Replace a compromised credential without altering physical identity or build record.

GATE-1.statement: Stages 9, 10, 11 are three independent gates; a unit must pass each to reach the next.

GATE-2.statement: Each gate has its own pass/fail and its own rework routing; a failure routes to a stage-specific disposition and re-enters at the correct point.
GATE-2.rationale: Preserves the 'why did it fail' signal for yield analysis as volume scales.

HW-1.statement: Hardware checks and pass criteria are determined by the ordered device model (configuration-driven).

HW-2.statement: Verify the built unit matches its ordered model recipe; flag missing expected hardware or unexpected hardware present.

MDL-1.statement: The product is offered as a handful of fixed models/variants, each with a known fixed hardware recipe; the system holds a small set of model recipes (no combinatorial configuration engine); new models added as new recipes.

CAL-1.statement: On successful calibration, capture and retain a full reproducible record: reference standards used (cert IDs and expiry), raw readings per standard, resulting coefficients, calibration equipment/fixture ID, operator, timestamp, environmental conditions, device firmware/SW version.

CAL-2.statement: Reference standards are tracked entities, each with cert ID, expiry, and identifier, so the calibration station can verify validity at the point of use.

CAL-3.statement: Calibration hard-stops (cannot begin or complete, no override) if any reference standard in use is expired or lacks a valid certificate.

CAL-4.statement: A unit may retry calibration up to 3 attempts; every attempt, including failed ones, is retained in the full internal record.

CAL-5.statement: On exceeding the retry cap, escalate to a supervisor disposition (route back to hardware stage 9, or scrap/quarantine); disposition reason logged; no indefinite retry.

CAL-6.statement: The shipped calibration certificate shows only the valid passing run; the full internal record retains all attempts.

QC-1.statement: QC is the final human sign-off on both the physical unit (condition, labeling, serial, accessories, seals) and its complete record (genealogy complete, certificate generated, all prior gates passed, regulatory docs present).

QC-2.statement: QC must verify the unit actually passed every prior required stage — a last guard against a record gap.

QC-3.statement: Passing QC finalizes the unit's record and authorizes the unit for stage 12 (cloud backup) and shipping.

BK-1.statement: After QC, the unit's complete factory record (genealogy, calibration record, certificate, test results) must be backed up to the cloud.

BK-2.statement: If cloud backup cannot complete, the unit is hard-blocked and cannot proceed to packaging/shipping until backed up; no override path.

SHIP-1.statement: Record packaged and shipped status with timestamp and operator; attach final shipping documents and the calibration certificate to the unit's record.

SHIP-2.statement: Destination, carrier, and tracking are out of scope for the factory system; reserved for a future fulfillment system.

SHIP-3.statement: Packaging/shipping stages must be architecturally separable so they can move into a dedicated fulfillment system later without disrupting the production core.

SHIP-4.statement: A unit's factory lifecycle is complete at 'shipped'; delivery, field use, and RMA are outside this system's scope.

RBAC-1.statement: Scrap/write-off of a calibrated unit or expensive sub-assembly is a high-cost action requiring the manager tier, not every shift supervisor.
RBAC-1.rationale: Scrapping a calibrated device or X-ray tube is a real financial and regulatory event.

RBAC-2.statement: QC sign-off is its own distinct named authority, not merely a permission every supervisor holds.

RBAC-3.statement: One person may hold multiple roles, except the system must enforce separation-of-duty on accountability points: the QC sign-off (and high-cost approvals such as scrap) on a unit cannot be the same person who did the work on that unit.
RBAC-3.rationale: If the assembler of a unit can sign off its QC, the sign-off is meaningless.

RBAC-4.statement: Separation-of-duty is enforced by default but may be waived by the manager tier for a specific unit with a recorded justification (who, why, which unit); a visible, reviewable exception, not routine.
RBAC-4.rationale: Prevents deadlock on thin staffing; waiver record doubles as a staffing signal.

AUD-1.statement: All retained records (overrides, re-allocations, every calibration attempt, gate results, waivers, sign-offs) must be complete and tamper-evident.

SCALE-1.statement: Support multiple units, stations, and operators at once; target ~10 devices/day, scaling to 100+.

RPT-1.statement: Management must be able to see where every unit is, which units are blocked and why (especially cloud-blocked at stages 7 and 12), and yield/failure data per gate.

IF-ORD-1.statement: Receive an approved order containing ORD-1 fields; be able to reject an invalid/un-buildable order upstream with a reason (ORD-2).

IF-INV-1.statement: Look up and bind serial+lot data for allocated parts.

IF-INV-2.statement: On release of an allocated serial, report the release reason code to the inventory module and do not change the part's availability.

IF-CLD-1.statement: Query and obtain the latest production SW/FW from the cloud (stage 7).

IF-CLD-2.statement: Enroll a device and obtain its cloud identity/credentials at provisioning (stage 8).

IF-CLD-3.statement: Back up a unit's complete record to the cloud and receive confirmation (stage 12).

IF-CLD-4.statement: Detect cloud/connectivity unavailability and surface it to the floor (stages 7 and 12 are hard-blocked on it).

separation_of_duty.rule: the signer/approver of a unit cannot be the person who did the work on that unit

tiers.may_do: perform the work of their stage

rework_loops.on_cap_exceeded: supervisor disposition to stage 9 or scrap

open_items_for_management_decision.question: May a single operator work more than one unit at the same time?
Should any future SW/FW update be flaggable as 'mandatory before ship' as an exception? Current decision is a uniform hard block with no exception.

decisions_against_recommendation.question: Q8c
decisions_against_recommendation.topic: escape path during stage-7 cloud outage

decision_log.choice: MES core with full per-unit traceability; digital twin/simulation deferred
Factory owns stages 3-14; eStore, cloud, inventory external; 13-14 separable
Serial-level, supported by incoming QC of parts
Supervisor re-allocation, logged; original + replacement kept in genealogy
Report reason code; inventory decides fate; factory never frees stock itself
Hard-stop on all five cases; no bench override
Pinned validated baseline first
Hard block, no override (documented tradeoff)
Created at provisioning; separate credential bound 1:1 to serial; rotatable
Three independent gates, each with own pass/fail and rework
Configuration-driven against the ordered model recipe
A handful of fixed models; unknown-model orders rejected
Full reproducible record retained
Hard-stop, no override; standards are tracked entities
Retry cap 3 then supervisor disposition; clean certificate, complete history
Final human sign-off on unit + record; finalizes and authorizes for ship
Hard block, no override (documented tradeoff)
Minimal close-out; logistics out of scope; separable
Three tiers; multiple roles per person; separation-of-duty enforced, manager-waivable
Sufficient-for-the-line set required; invalid/unknown-model orders rejected
